name: Bento
summary: Warm peach + soft blue on cream, modular grid cards, trendy tech

color_palette.secondary: #60A5FA (soft blue for secondary accents and data)

typography.heading_font: Be Vietnam Pro Bold/SemiBold (or Inter/Plus Jakarta Sans for full Bento aesthetic)
typography.vietnamese_diacritics: Good with Be Vietnam Pro; Inter has acceptable but not purpose-built Vietnamese support; Plus Jakarta Sans adequate but not optimized

animation_style.approach: Playful, staggered card reveals - each bento tile animates in with slight delay, scale-up with spring
animation_style.entrance_pattern: Cards scale from 0.9 to 1.0 with fade-in, staggered by 3-5 frames per card; parallax subtle hover effects
animation_style.spring_config: Damping 10-12, mass 0.6, stiffness 100 (slightly bouncy, playful feel)
animation_style.motion_budget: Medium-high - the grid layout invites animation of individual tiles, which adds visual interest but risks being distracting

projector_readability.contrast_approach: Dark text on cream/white backgrounds; pastel accents (peach, soft blue) may wash out on projectors
projector_readability.min_font_sizes: Body 28px, headings 36px+ adequate but stat numbers at 64-72px are the real visual anchors
projector_readability.color_washout: WARNING: Soft blue (#60A5FA) and warm peach (#F97316) at reduced saturation may lose identity when projected. Peach-orange is more resilient than soft blue. Cream background (#FFFBF0) projects cleanly.
projector_readability.line_weight: Card borders typically 1px with shadow - thin borders WILL disappear on projectors; need to increase to 2px solid

wcag_contrast_score.peach_on_cream: #F97316 on #FFFBF0 = 3.1:1 (FAIL for small text, AA pass for large text only)
wcag_contrast_score.blue_on_cream: #60A5FA on #FFFBF0 = 2.8:1 (FAIL for text - use only for decorative/icon purposes)
wcag_contrast_score.overall: Body text passes AAA; accent colors fail for functional text and must be limited to large headings, icons, or decorative elements

projector_degradation_resistance.details: The soft, pastel nature of Bento palettes is designed for high-quality screens (Retina displays, modern monitors), not projectors. Soft blue washes out significantly. Peach-orange loses warmth and may read as generic orange. Card shadows (typical in Bento) become invisible on projectors. The cream background itself is fine. Grid gaps and card separation rely on subtle shadows that projectors eliminate - need solid borders instead.

vietnamese_diacritics_rendering.font_suitability: Good if using Be Vietnam Pro; risk of degradation if switching to Inter or Plus Jakarta Sans which have less optimized Vietnamese glyph design
vietnamese_diacritics_rendering.line_height_needs: line-height 1.5-1.6; bento cards with constrained heights may clip diacritics if not careful
vietnamese_diacritics_rendering.min_size_for_clarity: 28px minimum; bento layouts tend to cram more items per frame, creating pressure to shrink text below safe thresholds

cultural_fit.alignment: Weak - Bento is a Japanese-inspired tech aesthetic with no Vietnamese cultural resonance
cultural_fit.details: The Bento grid style is associated with Apple WWDC, Silicon Valley product pages, and tech startups. Peach and soft blue evoke modern SaaS dashboards, not Vietnamese academic or political content. For a topic on national unity (đại đoàn kết toàn dân tộc), this palette and style feel disconnected from the subject matter.
cultural_fit.risk: High - teacher may perceive a mismatch between trendy tech aesthetics and serious political/academic content; could seem like prioritizing style over substance

cultural_semiotics.peach_orange: Tech warmth, Silicon Valley friendliness, startup energy - no Vietnamese cultural meaning
cultural_semiotics.soft_blue: Trust, technology, calmness - generic corporate/tech semiotics
cultural_semiotics.bento_grid: Japanese lunch box organization metaphor, adopted by Apple and tech companies - foreign cultural reference
cultural_semiotics.overall: Semiotics point entirely to tech industry; zero Vietnamese or socialist/political symbolism

teacher_perception_signal.signal: Technically skilled but potentially misaligned priorities
teacher_perception_signal.details: Teacher sees students with strong design/tech skills (positive) but may question why a political science presentation looks like a tech product page (negative). The trendiness could be interpreted as caring more about aesthetics than academic content.
teacher_perception_signal.effort_perception: High technical effort recognized, but cultural appropriateness may be questioned

rubric_alignment.format_15pct: Strong for format points - modular grid is visually organized and impressive
rubric_alignment.theory_35pct: Weak - bento tiles encourage bite-sized info chunks that may fragment theoretical arguments; dense theory doesn't fit naturally in small cards
rubric_alignment.application_50pct: Medium - stat numbers and data viz work well in bento tiles, but narrative application content is harder to present in modular chunks

content_density_capacity.words_per_frame: 60-90 words - bento layout distributes content across multiple small cards, reducing per-card density
content_density_capacity.data_viz_support: Good for stats and numbers (hero stat tiles), medium for complex charts (space constraints in grid cells)
content_density_capacity.theory_density: Low-medium - the modular nature fragments long theoretical arguments; better for bullet points than paragraphs

production_effort.migration_cost: High - requires complete redesign from current card-based editorial layout to modular grid system. Need to implement CSS Grid with variable-size tiles, rethink information architecture per frame, redesign all section components.
production_effort.component_changes: Major - need new BentoGrid container, BentoTile components with size variants (1x1, 2x1, 1x2, 2x2), stat tiles, icon tiles. Existing GlassPanel/SectionTitle need significant rework.

remotion_implementation_complexity.css_needs: Complex - CSS Grid with named areas, responsive tile sizing, shadow/blur effects (though shadows should be replaced with borders for projector)
remotion_implementation_complexity.threejs_usage: 3D background should be removed - Bento style is flat/2D by nature
remotion_implementation_complexity.render_performance: Medium - multiple animated tiles per frame increases render computation; staggered animations need careful frame counting
remotion_implementation_complexity.overall: High complexity - fundamentally different layout paradigm from current codebase

distraction_level: Medium-high - the grid animation and multiple tiles competing for attention can distract from content, especially with staggered reveals
differentiation: High - no other student group will use Bento-style layouts for political science; stands out dramatically but may stand out for wrong reasons

temporal_coherence.consistency: Medium - grid layout provides structural consistency but tile arrangements will vary significantly per section, potentially feeling inconsistent
temporal_coherence.section_transitions: Challenging - transitioning between different grid configurations may feel jarring
temporal_coherence.speaker_differentiation: Can assign tile colors per speaker but this fragments the already-complex grid further

dark_vs_light_mode_viability: Primarily light mode. Bento can work dark (Apple WWDC style) but pastel accents lose their character on dark backgrounds. Light cream is the canonical Bento surface.

examples: Apple WWDC 2023-2024 feature slides - dark Bento grid with product feature tiles
Linear.app landing page - Bento-style feature grid
Notion feature marketing pages - tinted Bento cards grouped by category
Vercel/Next.js landing pages - modular grid layouts
bentogrids.com - curated gallery of Bento grid implementations
precedent.notes: All precedents are from tech/product contexts. No examples found of Bento style used for academic, political, or Vietnamese cultural presentations. This would be a first-of-its-kind application.
